Question: Period aggregation windows (quarterly / yearly)
Description: Monthly RA inputs (361 months: CRD, RA STAT, RA FI, RE) must be collapsed into the quarterly and yearly points of the output term structure, and the method differs by measure type. CRD is a stock (an exposure level) so it is AVERAGED over the months of the period: quarterly = mean of the 3 months in the quarter (M[3q-2..3q]); yearly = mean of the window. The RA metrics (RA STAT, RA FI, RE) accrue through the period so they are SUMMED with a half-weight (trapezoidal) window that shares the boundary months between adjacent periods: Q1 = M1 + M2/2; Qn = M[3q-4]/2 + M[3q-3] + M[3q-2] + M[3q-1]/2; yearly = plain sums with Y1 = 6 months (stub) and Yn = 12. Code: PrimaryView.aggregate. CATCH: the CRD window (M[3q-2..3q], inside the quarter) and the RA window (M[3q-4..3q-1], shifted ~1 month earlier across the boundary) are OFFSET by about a month. Harmless at normal terms, but at the deep-tail run-off cliff (~term 30 / Q30) the misaligned windows straddle the discontinuity differently, RA can exceed 1, the cumulative product flips negative and the tail blows up (e.g. INVEST -389) - which is why Q30 added a run-off freeze guard. NEEDED FROM BUSINESS: (1) confirm the methodology - CRD = average (level), RA = half-weight trapezoidal sum (accrual); (2) confirm the yearly stub Y1 = 6 months, Yn = 12; (3) decide whether the CRD-vs-RA window offset is intentional (keep it and rely on the Q30 guard) or whether the windows should be ALIGNED to the same months (likely removes the tail spike at source). Point (3) is the genuinely open item - Q30 lists Q7 as a dependency.
Engine behaviour today: Quarterly: RA metrics half-weight window, CRD block mean of 3 months. Yearly: sums; CRD mean.
What we need from the business: Confirm windows. NOTE: CRD and RA-metric windows are offset, which causes the tail spike in Q30.

Question: Flat-tail boundary
Description: The output term structure reports EAD_RA_RATE on a fixed grid (quarterly 0, 0.25, ... 50.0, 50.25, then 100; yearly 0, 1, ... 50, then 100), but the RA input only has 361 monthly points (~30 years), so the curve can only be COMPUTED to a limited term. Q9 sets what happens beyond that: compute to the last computable term, then HOLD that last value FLAT out to the reporting horizon (50) and the tail point (100). With 361 months the last fully-windowed term is 29.75 (quarterly) / 29 (yearly) - the quarterly window for term 30 (period 121) needs month 362, which is past the data, so it cannot be computed. Code: PrimaryView.computeRa stops when the term exceeds COMPUTED_HORIZON_Y=30 or a period window falls off the end of the data (returns None), so the series is only as long as the computable prefix; PrimaryView.termSeries then maps each grid term with idx = min(n, length)-1, clamping to the last computed value = the flat fill. Grid sizes: 203 (Q: coreMax 50.25 -> 202 core points + 100) and 52 (Y: 51 + 100). Same None-stops-the-series mechanism also drives the run-off freeze (Q30, RUNOFF_RA_CAP), so the last computable term can be shortened by run-off, not just by data length. NEEDED FROM BUSINESS: (1) confirm the extrapolation rule - hold the last computed value flat (vs extrapolate a decay/trend, run to zero, or cap at a fixed projection horizon); (2) confirm the reporting horizon - flat to 50 then a single 100 point (the meaning of the 100 point is Q23); (3) note that with the current 361-month file the real computed tail is 29.75/29 (a longer vintage reaches 30) - behaviour unchanged, but the last-real-then-flat boundary shifts with input length.
Engine behaviour today: Compute to the last computable term, then hold flat to 50 and 100.
What we need from the business: Confirm the flat-tail rule.

Question: Stress leg selection (+ / -) under FWL=YES
Description: For FWL=YES matrices the RA input provides two stress legs, STRESS (+) and STRESS (-), on top of BASELINE, and the scenario RA is computed by blending baseline toward ONE stress leg - so the engine must decide which leg each scenario (Adverse / Optimistic / Extreme) leans toward. Q10 is that selection rule: choose the leg by the SIGN of the scenario's rate move vs Central, where delta = MACRO[scenario][date] - MACRO[Central][date]; delta < 0 -> STRESS (-), delta >= 0 -> STRESS (+). Code: PrimaryView.scenarioRa - 'val (fs, rs) = if (delta < 0) (fm, rm) else (fp, rp)'. delta is the macro rate gap for the matrix's MACRO_VARIABLE, read from the scenario path via deltaPath/macroDeltaArray, and is evaluated PER PERIOD, so a path that changes sign can switch legs term by term; the chosen leg is then blended with weight |delta|/ref_shock (magnitude is Q12). Why the direction is correct (evidence, target MORTGAGE_Q): STRESS(-)-baseline RAISES RA, STRESS(+)-baseline LOWERS it; the target's Adverse/Extreme show a positive RA shock (rates fall -> delta<0 -> STRESS(-)) and Optimistic a negative shock (rates rise -> delta>=0 -> STRESS(+)) - all three match. Caveats that are NOT flaws in this rule: distinguishing Adverse from Extreme needs different macro deltas (data, Q15); the shock MAGNITUDE is ref_shock (Q12) - Q10 only sets which leg/direction. NEEDED FROM BUSINESS: (1) confirm the leg-by-sign convention and the tie-break at delta=0 (currently STRESS(+); immaterial since the blend weight is 0 there); (2) confirm the directional mapping is economically intended (a downward-rate scenario pulls toward STRESS(-) and raises RA); (3) note the dependencies - A-vs-E differentiation (Q15) and shock magnitude (Q12) are separate confirmations.
Engine behaviour today: delta<0 -> STRESS(-); delta>=0 -> STRESS(+), by sign of the macro rate move.
What we need from the business: Confirm leg-by-sign rule.

Question: Macro shock: single date or path over a window?
Description: The FWL=YES shock blends RA toward a stress leg by an amount driven by how far a scenario's macro rate sits from Central (the delta). Q11 decides WHICH macro reading drives it: a single projection date (one shock for the whole curve) or the whole macro PATH over a window (a delta that varies term by term). DECISION: use the path over a window shock_window_start..shock_window_end; term 0 = window start; step = 1 quarter (quarterly) or 1 year = 4 quarters (yearly); past the window end the last delta is held flat. Replaces the earlier single projection_date design. Code: macroDeltaArray builds delta_q = MACRO[scenario][q] - MACRO[Central][q] for the matrix's MACRO_VARIABLE over each quarter of shockWindow; deltaPath turns it into a per-period function (period 1 -> window start, advance 1 quarter (Q) or 4 (Y), min(...,length-1) holds the last delta past the window); PrimaryView.scenarioRa consumes deltaAt(period) to pick the leg (Q10) and weight the blend, so a ramping path gives a term-varying shock. WHY a path not a single date: at a single near date all scenarios are still equal -> delta=0 -> no shock; the path lets the delta ramp in as scenarios diverge, growing the shock with term. RESULT: mechanism verified (Adverse/Extreme lower EAD, Optimistic higher); numeric match unchanged because the magnitude is tiny (ref_shock=1.0, Q12) and the tail-vintage error dominates (Q15) - separate issues, not a flaw in the path. UPDATE 2026-06-04: with Scenario_EAD_FWD.xlsx (coverage 2025Q1..2028Q4) the window moved to 2025Q4..2028Q4; the new scenario is forward-looking (no long history) so the term-0/window anchor must be re-confirmed (Q25); mechanism unchanged. Config: tseadfwd_app.shock_window_start / shock_window_end. NEEDED FROM BUSINESS: (1) confirm path-over-window (not single-date) is intended; (2) confirm the term-0 anchor and window bounds for the forward-looking file (2025Q4..2028Q4 is the current assumption) - see Q25; (3) confirm the hold-last-delta rule past the window end (vs revert to 0 or extrapolate); (4) note dependencies - shock magnitude (Q12) and Adverse-vs-Extreme (Q15) are separate sign-offs; Q11 only governs the source/shape of the delta.
Engine behaviour today: Macro path over a shock window; term 0 = window start, step 1 quarter.
What we need from the business: Confirm the window anchor for the forward-looking file (see Q25).
